settings.query:
MATCH (r:Router)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(c:City)
RETURN c.region AS region, r.role AS role, count(*) AS routeurs
ORDER BY region, role

settings.markdown:
## WAN Digital Twin
Le WAN de la banque (20 villes, 44 routeurs) comme graphe Neo4j.

Couche physique : Router → Interface → LINK ; couche derivee : CONNECTED_TO (utilisee par toutes les analyses).

settings.query:
MATCH (a:Router {role:'CORE'})-[r:CONNECTED_TO]-(b:Router {role:'CORE'})
WHERE a.hostname < b.hostname
RETURN a, r, b

MATCH ()-[l:LINK]->()
RETURN l.medium AS support, count(*) AS liens ORDER BY support

latestCypherQueries.query:
CYPHER 25
WITH [$neodash_failed_router] AS failed
MATCH (hq:Router {role:'CORE'})-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:City {code:'PAR'})
WHERE NOT hq.hostname IN failed
WITH failed, collect(hq) AS hqs
MATCH (r:Router)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(c:City)
WHERE NOT r.hostname IN failed
WITH failed, hqs, c, collect(r) AS cityRouters
WHERE NONE(r IN cityRouters WHERE EXISTS {
  MATCH (r)(()-[:CONNECTED_TO]-(mid:Router WHERE NOT mid.hostname IN failed))*(hq)
  WHERE hq IN hqs
})
RETURN c.code AS ville, c.name AS nom,
       [r IN cityRouters | r.hostname] AS routeurs_isoles
ORDER BY ville

CYPHER 25
WITH [$neodash_failed_router, 'uytxutfu', 'PAR-CORE-01'] AS failed
MATCH (hq:Router {role:'CORE'})-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:City {code:'PAR'})
WHERE NOT hq.hostname IN failed
WITH failed, collect(hq) AS hqs
MATCH (r:Router)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(c:City)
WHERE NOT r.hostname IN failed
WITH failed, hqs, c, collect(r) AS cityRouters
WHERE NONE(r IN cityRouters WHERE EXISTS {
  MATCH (r)(()-[:CONNECTED_TO]-(mid:Router WHERE NOT mid.hostname IN failed))*(hq)
  WHERE hq IN hqs
})
RETURN c.code AS ville, c.name AS nom,
       [r IN cityRouters | r.hostname] AS routeurs_isoles
ORDER BY ville

settings.markdown:
### Simulation de panne
Choisissez un routeur : les villes qui perdent toute connectivite vers le siege (Paris) apparaissent ci-dessous.

Essayez `PAR-CORE-01` (Johannesburg isolee) — puis la table des **paires critiques** montre ce que votre supervision ne voit pas : les doubles pannes.

widgets.title: Paires CORE critiques (double panne isolant au moins une ville)

settings.query:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (c1:Router {role:'CORE'}), (c2:Router {role:'CORE'})
WHERE c1.hostname < c2.hostname
WITH [c1.hostname, c2.hostname] AS failed
CALL (failed) {
  MATCH (hq:Router {role:'CORE'})-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:City {code:'PAR'})
  WHERE NOT hq.hostname IN failed
  WITH failed, collect(hq) AS hqs
  WHERE size(hqs) > 0
  MATCH (r:Router)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(c:City)
  WHERE NOT r.hostname IN failed
  WITH failed, hqs, c, collect(r) AS cityRouters
  WHERE NONE(r IN cityRouters WHERE EXISTS {
    MATCH (r)(()-[:CONNECTED_TO]-(mid:Router WHERE NOT mid.hostname IN failed))*(hq)
    WHERE hq IN hqs
  })
  RETURN collect(c.code) AS villes_isolees
}
WITH failed, villes_isolees WHERE size(villes_isolees) > 0
RETURN failed AS paire_core, villes_isolees
ORDER BY size(villes_isolees) DESC, paire_core

CYPHER 25
WITH [$neodash_failed_router] AS failed
MATCH (hq:Router {role:'CORE'})-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:City {code:'PAR'})
WHERE NOT hq.hostname IN failed
WITH failed, collect(hq) AS hqs
MATCH (r:Router)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(c:City)
WHERE NOT r.hostname IN failed
WITH failed, hqs, c, collect(r) AS cityRouters
WHERE NONE(r IN cityRouters WHERE EXISTS {
  MATCH (r)(()-[:CONNECTED_TO]-(mid:Router WHERE NOT mid.hostname IN failed))*(hq)
  WHERE hq IN hqs
})
RETURN count(c)

CALL () {
  CALL gds.graph.drop('wan_dash_yens', false) YIELD graphName
  RETURN count(graphName) AS dropped
}
CALL gds.graph.project('wan_dash_yens', 'Router', {CONNECTED_TO: {orientation: 'UNDIRECTED', properties: 'latency_ms'}}) YIELD graphName
MATCH (src:Router)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:City {code: $neodash_src_city})
WITH graphName, src ORDER BY src.hostname LIMIT 1
MATCH (dst:Router)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:City {code: $neodash_dst_city})
WITH graphName, src, dst ORDER BY dst.hostname LIMIT 1
CALL gds.shortestPath.yens.stream(graphName, {
  sourceNode: src, targetNode: dst, k: 3, relationshipWeightProperty: 'latency_ms'
}) YIELD index, totalCost, nodeIds
RETURN index + 1 AS route, [id IN nodeIds | gds.util.asNode(id).hostname] AS sauts,
       totalCost AS latence_ms

CALL () {
  CALL gds.graph.drop('wan_dash_br', false) YIELD graphName
  RETURN count(graphName) AS dropped
}
CALL gds.graph.project('wan_dash_br', 'Router', {CONNECTED_TO: {orientation: 'UNDIRECTED'}}) YIELD graphName
CALL gds.bridges.stream(graphName) YIELD from, to
RETURN gds.util.asNode(from).hostname AS extremite_a, gds.util.asNode(to).hostname AS extremite_b
ORDER BY extremite_a, extremite_b

settings.markdown:
### Fragilites calculees
Points d'articulation et ponts sont calcules par algorithme (GDS), pas tenus a la main.

En dessous : diversite de chemins entre deux villes — plus court chemin exact (Cypher) et 3 routes alternatives (Yen).

settings.query:
CALL () {
  CALL gds.graph.drop('wan_dash_ap', false) YIELD graphName
  RETURN count(graphName) AS dropped
}
CALL gds.graph.project('wan_dash_ap', 'Router', {CONNECTED_TO: {orientation: 'UNDIRECTED'}}) YIELD graphName
CALL gds.articulationPoints.stream(graphName) YIELD nodeId
RETURN gds.util.asNode(nodeId).hostname AS routeur_spof ORDER BY routeur_spof

CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Router)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:City {code: $neodash_src_city}),
      (b:Router)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:City {code: $neodash_dst_city})
MATCH p = SHORTEST 1 (a)-[:CONNECTED_TO]-+(b)
RETURN a.hostname AS de, b.hostname AS vers,
       [n IN nodes(p) | n.hostname] AS sauts,
       reduce(total = 0.0, r IN relationships(p) | total + r.latency_ms) AS latence_ms
ORDER BY latence_ms ASC
LIMIT 1

settings.markdown:
### Incident P1 pose sur le graphe
Creez l'incident de demo en executant l'etape 1 de `cypher/demo/Q6_incident_rca.cypher` (Browser). L'incident, son impact et la topologie vivent dans le meme graphe.

Reset : bloc commente en fin de Q6.

settings.query:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (:Incident)-[:AFFECTS]->(fr:Router)
WITH collect(DISTINCT fr.hostname) AS failed
MATCH (hq:Router {role:'CORE'})-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:City {code:'PAR'})
WHERE NOT hq.hostname IN failed
WITH failed, collect(hq) AS hqs
MATCH (r:Router)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(c:City)
WHERE NOT r.hostname IN failed
WITH failed, hqs, c, collect(r) AS cityRouters
WHERE NONE(r IN cityRouters WHERE EXISTS {
  MATCH (r)(()-[:CONNECTED_TO]-(mid:Router WHERE NOT mid.hostname IN failed))*(hq)
  WHERE hq IN hqs
})
RETURN c.code AS ville_impactee, c.name AS nom,
       [r IN cityRouters | r.hostname] AS routeurs_isoles
ORDER BY ville_impactee

MATCH (i:Incident)-[:AFFECTS]->(r:Router)
RETURN i.id AS incident, i.severity AS severite, i.type AS type,
       toString(i.started_at) AS debut, r.hostname AS routeur_affecte, r.status AS statut
ORDER BY incident

MATCH (i:Incident)-[a:AFFECTS]->(r:Router)
OPTIONAL MATCH (r)-[c:CONNECTED_TO]-(n:Router)
RETURN i, a, r, c, n
